Dr. Gurney is probably the most skilled Chiropractor I have visited in 30 years! However, he is a bit expensive, his staff isn't very friendly, and when I had a billing issue they weren't helpful in helping me resolve it. I don't think I will be going back.

Aspen Chiropractic is an excellent place for treatment. I have seen quite a few chiropractors, and I think Dr. Gurney is definitely one of the better ones in terms of knowledge and understanding of the human body/alignment. I’ve had chiropractors make adjustments prior to properly aligning my body. Dr. Gurney is very good at finding the places that require adjustment. He uses a unique method, which they call muscle testing, in order to find the areas of the body that require realignment. This is a technique that I’d never experienced before, but after experiencing it, I can find it very valid. It is my understanding that this muscle testing isn’t generally covered by insurance (my insurance doesn’t cover it)… I saw that the reviewer below didn’t want to pay the $12 for the muscle testing… yeah, money doesn’t grow on trees, but the muscle testing is worth 12 bucks if you are going to a chiropractor. I had a pulled hamstring that was highly problematic, and I had no idea that I was pulling it more and more, worsening the condition (I’m a yogi), until I’d had the muscle testing. It’s solved a lot of problems. I also appreciate that Dr. Gurney is very experienced with extremities. I have relatively flat feet, and my feet would not hold in alignment because of the poor condition of the muscles. In a matter of under 10 visits, my flat feet are now holding the correct form. My pulled hamstring is almost all healed (there is some inflammation at the points that attach to the bone… to be expected). My back feels great, and I’m in the mountains backpacking/hiking/climbing almost every weekend, so I’m not taking it easy at all. Dr. Gurney is great. I was very disheartened to see my other reviewer did not have as good of an experience, but please don’t let that thwart you… he’s a very good doctor. I can see where he can come off as a little flustered (per the other reviewer), but I think this is more a reflection of having so much information to offer, and not a lack of preparation. There have been a few times I needed to remind him to check my feet, but all it took was the reminder, and then he took care of it. If you are like me, and pretty much “captain active,” he’s quite excellent with that. I’ve also seen him very effectively heal injury-related mis-alignments (through friends’ experiences, not my own). Overall, I’d highly recommend Dr. Gurney.

Dr. Gurney was a joke. I was recommended to him, so thought I would be in capable hands, but his care was unacceptable. He was flustered while asking questions about where my pain was and then didn't take x-rays to check my spine to make informed decisions about treatment. He kept suggesting additional treatments that "insurance doesn't cover" and some of it was seriously ridiculous. He wanted to use a "muscular strength test" to determine what adjustments to make because regular adjustments "aren't as exact." I asked him to tell me what it involved before I paid for it and he said "It's hard to explain, I have to show you to really explain it, but it helps me be more exact and know where to move the bones." The test involved him pushing muscles before making adjustments - so I told him I didn't see how it was different than a standard adjustment should be, and said I wasn't interested. He continued to explain it more, I continued to say I don't think it does anything, so I'd rather he didn't.

My neck was really tight when he tried to adjust it, so when it didn't crack on the first try, he proceeded to force it THREE MORE TIMES. It was very painful and never adjusted. After the sub-par "adjustments" of my neck and upper back, he tried to set me up for another appointment; so I asked if he was going to check the rest of back and joints and do a full examination. He was surprised and said, "Oh yes, let's go ahead do that now."

When I left, the receptionist charged me for the muscle test I told him I didn't want, and twice for the exam and adjustment, saying they aren't the same....I told her I didn't want the muscle test and she said, next time I can make a note for him to not include it, but he did perform it this time. He is clearly incompetent and trying to squeeze every penny out of patients. I would not recommend him to anyone.
Not impressed.

My wife and I contacted attorney Williams as we wanted to update our trust, discuss elder law issues and Medicaid concerns. Our first interaction was via phone and attorney Williams was very thorough and answered many of our questions to the point that we felt confident with his legal expertise and command of Elder law issues. We scheduled an in-person meeting which was extremely instructive with regard to our options, pros and cons of those options and follow up steps. Attorney William's is incredibly well prepared and gave us a list of items/documents and templates we needed to gather to complete the process. We followed up with a second in-person meeting where he explained each and every page  listed in the trust, power of attorney, advanced health care directives, trust transfer documents, etc. Attorney Williams wanted to ensure complete accuracy and our full understanding of the components of the trust. This was crucial for us as we wanted our last wishes to be fully represented in this estate planning process. We are very appreciative of attorney William's vast knowledge, kindness, professionalism and expediency. We fully recommend attorney Williams to expertly manage any issues related to elder law, Medicaid and estate planning.

Tim Williams was recommended to us by a friend that my son worked with. His family had dealt with the same issues that we were having, So we decided to give Tim a call and it was the best decision I ever made. I was trying to take care of my wife dementia on my own and it was finally taking it's toll on me, so I just had to do something to help my wife (Rosie) and give me some peace of mind. When my son met with Tim he explained everything in detail to make it easy for us to understand and how difficult some things could be. We did what he suggested and was able to my wife on medicaid and the help she needed. Tim became her advocate to do the medicaid yearly review. My wife is doing fine, but her memory has slightly gotten worse, but she still remember who we are and then COVID happened and we could only do window visits or talk to her on the phone, which was hard. Now we can visit again and even take her out to the park or home or just drive her around so she can relax and she different places. We are very thankful that Tim was there for us. I would give him 10 stars if I could.

Thanks

John Norman Sr.
I was overwhelmed with a Nursing home, Medicaid matter and called Timothy. He helped ease my mind and put things in a way that I could understand and was very helpful.

I reached out to Tim regarding a situation with my mother and long term care.  There was a lot of anxiety regarding her financial situation.  Tim called me and was willing to talk to me for several minutes asking about the situation.  He then provided me the insight I needed to put my mind at ease.  He was very cordial to talk to and very informed.  Thank you Tim!

My mother became ill last year and needed to be moved to a long—term health care facility. My son referred Tim Williams to us to hire as an attorney. With everything going on, his expertise and hard work gave us the ability to navigate a tough situation.

He was able to help us through the Medicaid process while still protecting her assets. He also drew us up a trust when we only had a will in place a week before she passed. He was always available on demand, and took time and care to explain what was happening and how to work through complex government regulations.

We couldn’t recommend his services more, if you find yourself or a loved one in similar situation, you will alleviate a lot of hassle and stress by hiring Tim.

For those looking for assistance from an good elder law attorney:

My initial contact with Tim Williams was quite by chance in finding a stray brochure at a local care facility.

At our first meeting Tim answered every question on my four pages before a I was even able to ask questions. Tim's knowledge of the Medicaid system helped me prepare for the extensive application process. He walked me through the frustrating process of gathering and submitting all the Medicaid system required to achieve eligibility for my loved one. His work for me didn't stop with eligibility as Tim was more than happy to assist me in problem solving that continued long after his financial obligation to me was complete.
Ray Lewis

I cannot say enough good about the work Tim Williams did for us as our Family Law Attorney.  He took a lot of time to explain all the details of the law to us concerning our family's needs concerning our trust.  He is an excellent Elder Law Attorney.

Tim Williams helped qualify my sister for Utah Medicaid after we moved her from Chicago.  Her asset base was somewhat substantial and her financial history more than a bit complicated.  Tim guided us through the exercise with precision.  He was thoroughly professional and very clear and specific in his instructions on our role in the lengthy, detailed process.  He did a great deal of the "grunt work" (and there was a lot of it.)   We always felt we were "on track."  Very impressive:  many times, I'd email Tim with a question late at night or on the weekend and he would get back to me within minutes.  I never once had to wait for feedback.

Mr. Williams was God send.  After attempting a very difficult process of getting my elderly mother on Medicaid in order to put her in a care center I found the process overwhelming. The stress had taken its toll. I started looking for someone to help in this process.  That is when I found Timothy Williams on the internet.  Being that there are many people out there that are just looking to make money off people who are desperate in these situations I call him to meet to discuss my mothers situation.  I found Mr. Williams to be a very knowledgeable and honest attorney who was upfront with information to help me in my task at hand.  Though stressful for me, he made the process look easy.  I was grateful to find him and have appreciated his help.

reviews.text: Worst experience I’ve had hiring an attorney. Assured me when he took my case that he’d be able to negotiate a plea in abeyance even though my speed was over the limit for that. reviews.ownerResponse: This person is referring to Weber Law. She was charged with speeding over 100 mph. She is upset her charge was not dismissed and does not understand the length of time it takes, on some cases, to negotiate a reasonable resolution with the prosecutor. As was explained to her, case negotiation is with the prosecutor, not the Judge, and as such, it is not uncommon for a case to run 3 to 4 months before a resolution is achieved. Negotiation is a process unless one accepts the first offer given by a prosecutor. Lastly, this person got a good resolution, and she agreed to it, which saved her money on car insurance costs along with other favorable considerations.

My husband and I went to Merrilee with the task of constructing trusts that would address our separate assets, and in fair manner, provide for my husband’s step children, his own children, and my children.  When my husband died, Merrilee carefully worked through every complicated step with my husband’s son to settle my husband’s estate.  And, I am at peace knowing that when my estate needs to be settled by my executor, all is in order.  Thank you, Merrilee!

reviews.text:
Respectfully, I wouldn’t touch this law firm with a thirty nine and a half foot pole.
I began my case at the end of April 2025 with Blaine Edwards. During the initial consultation, I felt confident moving forward. As time went on, he was frequently late to calls and even missed a few completely. After I addressed how important timing was, things improved, but that conversation shouldn’t have been necessary in the first place!
I understand custody cases involve waiting and back-and-forth with the opposing party. What was far more frustrating was the firm’s communication. They encouraged using their online portal, yet I rarely received responses there. I was also given a number to text, but messages went to the general office and usually resulted in “we’ll talk to your attorney and get back to you,” without follow up.
The last time I heard from Blaine was beginning of August. Then, I didn’t hear from anyone for three weeks. In the midst of an already stressful situation, the silence was infuriating! All of a sudden, Alex Scherf overtook my case and there was no explanation on why. He was fine but not the attorney I originally agreed to work with. After my case was closed, I had an employee reach out to ask about my experience - I was very honest and open. I was told that Blaine left their firm in August and no longer employed by Hoyer. This was the first I had heard this! He then explained a lot was thrown at Alex at that time, which I completely understand! But there should’ve been a conversation and solution to actually manage Alex’s workload.
Hearing from Hoyer first was rare - I was the one constantly reaching out. When I asked for an update I felt reprimanded because they have a “24-48 hour response policy” okay it’s been 7 days since I’ve last heard from my attorney so?? I received several invoices that stated “various” for thousands of dollars. After my case had closed, they let me know that detailed billing notes were only visible on a computer browser not a phone, something I was never informed of. I know there are a lot of moving parts but even when I wasn’t hearing from anyone and there wasn’t any action, I would suddenly get a huge bill.
I wish I would’ve ditched Hoyer early on in my case. Overall, I felt like I was on the back burner the entire time, they didn’t care to fight for me, their communication poor, and the billing vague. I just finished my case and it took 10 months - in the initial consult, Blaine stated we could get things done in ~30 days. I understand some delays are outside the firm’s control, but there were certainly areas that could’ve been handled more proactively!
In the post-case call, I was offered my final invoice to be waived if I would leave a 5 star review… the total invoice was allegedly $400, which is a small amount in comparison to what I had already been charged in the previous 10 months. The offer itself was shocking!! I let them know I was uncomfortable leaving a dishonest review and to send me the final invoice. The next day I received a bill for $754, that’s fun that they found an additional $354 worth of made up work to charge me for! This firm is very slimy! I’m sure they will respond and explain everything away but take my word for it and use any other firm!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your perspective. Family law matters are often stressful and emotionally difficult, and we recognize that clients may experience the process differently.

For readers evaluating our firm, a few points may be helpful. Custody and divorce cases rarely resolve in 30 days, as timelines depend heavily on court scheduling, negotiations with the opposing party, and the pace of litigation. While we work to move cases forward as efficiently as possible, many factors are outside a firm’s control.

Our office maintains detailed, time-stamped records of communications and billing through our case management system. Client messages, calls, and portal activity are tracked so attorneys and staff can respond and keep work moving forward. Billing entries are also fully itemized within the client portal.

Attorney transitions can occur when a lawyer departs a firm. When that happens, cases are reassigned to another qualified attorney so representation continues without interruption.

As the person responsible for leading this team, I will add that it is disappointing to see our staff described in insulting terms. Our attorneys and support staff work extremely hard to treat every client with professionalism and respect, even when situations are stressful or emotions run high. Regardless of circumstances, our team has always approached this matter respectfully.

We regret when a client feels dissatisfied with their experience. Our team remains committed to professional representation, transparent billing, and responsive communication for every client we serve, and we wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your perspective. Family law cases are often emotionally difficult, and clients sometimes hope for outcomes that the facts of the case or the law ultimately do not support. While our attorneys work diligently to explore every available option, legal results are determined by the facts, the law, and the court, not by a law firm.

For readers evaluating our firm, it may also be helpful to understand that last year we experienced an unexpected attorney transition when an attorney left the firm. Several cases had to be reassigned quickly so clients would not experience a lapse in representation. Our team stepped in, reviewed files, and worked to continue moving matters forward during that transition. Electronic time stamps prove that there was no lag in representation.

In some situations, we take extra steps to help clients continue their case despite complications or changing circumstances. Unfortunately, even those efforts do not always lead to a result that satisfies the client.

While we regret when someone leaves the process dissatisfied, our firm remains committed to professional representation, transparent billing, and respectful communication for every client we serve. We wish you the best moving forward.
Thanks for your review Stephen!

reviews.text: Beware. They overcharge. The service and product I received was poor.  It cost me $931.50 for a simple demand letter that I could not use. The original charge was over $1,500.  Including three hours at $390/hr for their staff to conference with the attorney and write a letter that I was told would take 1 to 1 1/2 hours. They brought it down after I complained and fired them. The letter was incorrect and incomplete.  They did offer to rewrite it at no charge after I complained, but I do not trust the staff at this point.  Found a better attorney who charges $500 for this service, although I did pay for the consult.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Cindy, thank you for your feedback. For those reading, a few clarifications:
• Drafts are always sent for client review because revisions and feedback are part of ensuring accuracy. The letter can't have been "all wrong," we're not inept, in fact we're very good at what we do. We collected information and used what the client sent in to write it. There is often a need to clear up information to get the details right.
• Preparing documents involves more than drafting—it includes opening the file and gathering information, which typically takes at least 1–1.5 hours on it's own. Cindy's expectation was her own, not what we communicated or promised.
• Our fees and retainers are clearly outlined in advance and reflect standard rates for family law firms in Utah. A phone call with the attorney for a quick revision would have cost Cindy significantly less than the $500 she's paying someone else to doctor up our letter and send it.
• The demand letter was an early attempt to resolve the case cost-effectively, though these letters often receive little response. In many cases, the next step is moving forward with full representation.
• We extended a courtesy credit to be mindful of Cindy’s budget; such credits are a gesture of goodwill, not an admission of fault.
• While not every budget aligns with our legal fees, for clients who value strong advocacy our rates are consistent with the level of service we provide. We're not for everyone though, and we understand that.

reviews.text: Casey, Missy and everyone at Hoyer Law were so amazing to work with. They were quick to respond and answered every question I had. They also have me information i didn't know i needed. I would definitely use them again.

reviews.text: I just wanted to give everybody my opinion about this law firm. I had the opportunity to talk to Blaine Edwards. We sat down and went over all my problems. I gave him all my evidence we went through everything and he told me he was going to move forward on the case. So I paid $5000 detainer for his legal help. I was charged $1700 before they even signed onto my case. Then I had 2 more attorneys sign on. To my case which Cost more money. they did not do what they said they were going to do. reviews.ownerResponse:
Brad, thank you for sharing your perspective. For those reading, we want to clarify how our process works. At Hoyer Law, the level of attorney involvement always matches the complexity of a case. Sometimes this requires more than one attorney to provide the defense a client is asking for, and fees are directly tied to the amount of work performed. Retainers are applied against this work, so how long they last depends entirely on the level of representation needed. When Mr Bosshardt came to us he said funds for his defense were no object, he wanted the full battalion behind him. We took that to assume he wanted full representation, but that does not mean we charged him frivolously. All our billings are detailed, transparent and justifiable.

We are also committed to maintaining a professional and respectful attorney–client relationship. In rare circumstances, when expectations and communication no longer align, we may decide it’s best to withdraw rather than continue representation, as was the case here.

Our fees and retainers are always clearly explained up front, and while we may not be the right fit for every budget, we remain dedicated to providing high-quality advocacy and clear communication for clients who value experienced representation.

reviews.text:
This review is an account of my highly disappointing experience with Hoyer Law Firm, and I hope it serves as a cautionary tale for anyone considering their services.

I was initially impressed by the personal connection I felt with the founder during our preliminary discussions about my child custody case. His empathetic approach led me to hire them, believing my case was as important to them as it was to me.

However, post-contract, the firm’s service drastically changed. What started as regular updates soon dwindled to infrequent communication, despite me being billed for every attempt I made to gain insights into my case's progress. Alarmingly, I wasn't introduced to my actual representative until the day of the court hearing, which is unacceptable.

On the day of the hearing, I found myself having to confirm if an attorney from Hoyer Law would even be present. The hearing resulted in a denial of my request, primarily because the firm failed to submit a vital document on time—a document that explained the justification for amending my divorce decree. Despite having ample time (over a month), they only managed to submit it on the morning of the hearing.

Despite promises that I wouldn’t be charged for resubmitting the necessary documents, I was later billed over $1,000 in vague fees. Overall, the preparation for court alone cost me an excess of $6,000, which, after consultations with other lawyers, appears to be exorbitant given the unclear breakdown of charges.

My years of professional interactions with trial lawyers with a job I previously had have led me to realize one harsh truth: to them, clients are merely numbers. These lawyers, adept at sales, leverage your vulnerability to maximize their profit. Their primary skills typically do not lie in legal expertise but in their ability to sell a narrative to the judge, jury, and especially to you.

I urge extreme caution with Hoyer Law and, frankly, any firm. The legal system often feels rigged, with law firms like these playing a significant role in its corruption.

I regret choosing Hoyer Law. They overcharged me, assigned me a lawyer whose courtroom skills were questionable at best, and showed a general lack of diligence and urgency, except when it involved billing me or when my retainer was running low.

For those seeking justice or adjustments in legal agreements, consider handling it yourself or thoroughly researching before committing to any firm. From my extremely negative experience, I have learned that most firms exploit desperation. Don't fall victim to their tactics. Consider hiring someone else and consult friends/family that have had a very positive interaction with their law firm. They do exist but in my opinion are extremely rare.

Update: Based on their recent response (See the firms response), I hope anyone considering this firm sees the reality and looks elsewhere. They ignored the overcharging and the delays they caused by failing to submit required paperwork, opting instead to attack the very clients who paid them thousands for subpar (if any) actual service. There are many excellent law firms out there, but this one definitely isn’t among them.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Nate,
I'm sorry that you we did not meet your expectations. Sounds like you have issues with lawyers in general and that your review was largely a convoluted opinion about all law firms. We can't really help that. We carefully and responsibly prosecute all cases in our care. We have multiple attorneys in our office, and from time to time another attorney must cover a hearing, it is common practice. We hear it a lot, people complain about not getting enough updates, and then they complain for being billed for updates. It's usually a general complaint about having to pay for a professional service altogether. We sincerely hope you can find the lawyer of your dreams someday. In the meantime, we recommend Legal Aid for discounted and/or free legal defense.

reviews.text:
I paid just around $3000 in attorney fees for 2 months of representation with no court appearances or filings… just emails with OC and phone calls without even meeting Mr. Hoyer in person.   He quickly referred me to his subordinate counsel for several weeks. I received such a large bill through two months, I made a change immediately and was so glad that I did.  When I requested correspondence between him and opposing counsel to try to substantiate some of the cost, he would not forward that to me.  You may want to make that inquiry if you ever decide that you want to retain him as I feel, this is highly irregular and far from transparent when you are paying for his services.   Mr. Hoyers office said they would only send emails to my new attorney if he requests them but, would not send them to me.  I asked on multiple occasions for a 10 minute appointment with Mr. Hoyer to place a name to a face and shake a hand after spending thousands of dollars the first few weeks and during the first month and was told he was ill or on vacation and just given the run around or in court.  I also paid the $300 initial one hour consultation, which was by phone and not in person.  I will tell you that my experience was a couple years ago in 2022 and I have utilized five attorneys for business and real estate transactions that were FANTASTIC.   Mr Hoyer not only did not place his client as first priority, but overcharged and was an inefficient in utilizing an associate inexperienced attorney to assist and charged and charged.  He was not transparent and would not share communications ie emails between he and opposing counsel when I need to address my situation… when I retained my next attorney, his open book policy was to CC all communications which I found refreshing and exceptionally transparent.  Do you due diligence here…my experience was challenging, costly and a nightmare.

I just read the follow up which was done months later.  I cannot imagine anyone having an issue with the Utah State Bar being copied on correspondence between hired attorneys.  My newly hired attorney did not have that problem and never has in 30 years.  I find that laughable that an attorney can speak about your case to your attorney but your attorney cannot share this with you but, you pay him for his time???!!!  You can be the judge.   Secondly, I was told specifically by Mr. Hoyer that he would be handling my case in our hour long PAID discussion by phone.  I paid a $3500 retainer immediately because of that.  Evidently, he changed his mind as I was pushed over to another attorney almost immediately other than correspondence between OC...and by their own admission...they handle cases after Covid without even meeting many of their clients.  If YOU want this kind of relationship with your attorney ...this is the law firm for YOU.   I never had a video call, Zoom call or personal meeting.  Just emails and telephone calls for over $3000 in a few weeks with no filing or court appearance.   Just a few emails and phone calls. The Hoyer Law firm response..."It just saves everyone time and the effort of coming into the office."  reflects what you are paying for here. If you want this kind of arm's length, distant representation that I experienced as well which seems the norm here...this is the law firm for YOU.   I feel this follow up by Hoyer Law speaks volumes and best of luck in your decision making.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We understand that legal services can be a significant investment, and we do our best to be transparent about costs from the beginning. Our attorneys dedicate years to education, training, and experience to provide the highest level of professional representation, and the value of that expertise goes beyond just time spent on a case—it includes strategy, knowledge, and advocacy to help clients navigate complex legal matters.

We also believe in fairness when it comes to billing, and we strive to ensure that our clients understand the work being done on their behalf. We are always happy to discuss any concerns about fees or services, but we also recognize that quality legal representation requires a level of commitment and trust in the process.

While we regret that your experience did not meet your expectations, we remain committed to providing honest, skilled legal counsel to those who value professional advocacy. If you have any unresolved concerns, we would be happy to discuss them further.

Analia Hoyer
General Manager

reviews.text: Was a waste of my time and money. Over 8k spent and had absolutely nothing to show for it. Felt like blaine Edward’s was working with opposing counsel instead of me. Was told during negations that I was better to sue my ex after the divorce for breach of contract, turns out I can’t because we settled on the in the agreement. Was also told in December it would only cost me another $1000 to finish my divorce. Well $1500 later still in the same place. Charged for work that wasn’t done. Also keep getting sent a bill that I paid, they won’t respond to an email, won’t answer when I call and won’t return a call so I can figure out why I’m getting sent a bill when I called in and paid it. Nothing but crooks. Edited so there was a problem with them processing my final payment. Instead of calling me and letting me know they sent an email. I tried calling them to figure it out but they would not answer or return my calls. They sent me to collections because I filed a complaint with the ethics board. reviews.ownerResponse:

There are times when the landscape of a case changes, and in such instances our attorneys do all that is procedurally necessary to recommend the next best recourse. We pride ourselves in having a fully transparent billing system, and our staff is constantly reaching out to clients with outstanding invoices to clarify and/or collect payment via text, phone and email. I have looked at your case, it was professionally prosecuted and resolved according to the laws and procedures required by the state of Utah. I can honestly say that if we conducted our business as you described above we would not be licensed or in business. Attorneys cannot bill for work not done, it's unethical. The attorneys at Hoyer Law hold to the highest standards of ethics.

Best of luck,
Analia Hoyer - General Manager
Hoyer Law Firm

reviews.ownerResponse: Jose, thank you for your honest and fair review. I apologize that service for your latest case was not up to par with our standards and your expectations. Admittedly, we had a long period where we struggled finding quality employees (like so many employers have between 2020-2022). We have cleaned up the roster and currently have an exceptional team working for us, who help us keep up the rigorous standards that we have set up for ourselves. This said, Hoyer Law has always stood by their clients, and if your attorney did not show up to a hearing (which I can count with less than one hand has happened in the history of our firm), they would have made amends an reached out to the prosecutor or opposing counsel and corrected the issue. There is always a chance at human error when you have many moving parts, but issues like this are always addressed when brought to our attention. That is why ultimately, your most recent case was also successfully resolved. We wish you the best going forward.

reviews.text: I was going through a divorce about 3 years and had no idea what I was doing. My ex took my daughter and moved to Cedar City while I was at work. I googled lawyers in the area and met Casey in person. Seemed like he was rooting for the good dads out there and was going to be on my side. After $10K and a couple of meetings with him and many emails, we were nowhere. Never talked to him after the initial meeting and one court meeting. I got another lawyer, a family friend luckily. He looked at the paper work that Casey submitted to the court and asked me “do you want to give up all decision rights (religion, medical decision, schools, etc.) to your ex?!” I said of course not!! I thought we were going for equal rights on decisions and as much parent time as I could get with the distance between us. Well apparently Casey was not on my side after all. I have all emails and court docs (because I shared with my other lawyer for advice while I was working with Casey) on file still between Casey’s team and me. So I was a real client. This is a warning to good fathers/mothers that want what’s best for their child and themselves. With my new lawyer, things were completed in 2 months and worked out best for both parties. I wasn’t dragged along to keep paying for every single email and call so he made a buck. I don’t normally write reviews, but felt like I needed to write this one. My lawyer actually said I should get a refund because of the lack of work that was done by Casey’s team. But I was done, because all I wanted was my time with my daughter and I got it!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Silver, we are sorry that this is your perception of our representation on your case.  In reviewing your case as an entire staff we found several discrepancies with your representation.  However, we are ethically bound to privacy of your case and will not discuss the details in this forum.  I do know that you came to me for an emergency hearing representation, and that my staff and myself jumped on your case immediately, stopping work on other cases to take care of yours, and represented you at the same level as we do all our clients.  The rest of our reviews don't lie.  Your numerous communications were answered in a timely manner by both attorney and staff.  We still have the physical and electronic file with accurate information that can be recalled at all times to verify the validity of your claims above.  We wish you the best going forward.

reviews.text:
I tried to apply for disability by myself & then w a different company before I found Barnes Disability Advocates.
Once I had contacted them all my needs were taken care of.  Any time I had questions they were right there to explain or help. They filed appeals quickly sometimes the same day we received denials. They helped me fill out all of the overwhelming paperwork. Helped me win SSI while we waited for the opportunity to appear before the judge. I had some tricky issues they were able to sort out to insure I didn't loose my work credits.They were there next to me when I appeared before the the judge for the hearing.  Advised me and with some minor changes we won. This process took almost 2 yrs w all the loopholes in the system. Grateful for them being on my side!!
Would always recommend them to be anyone's advocate for disability!!

reviews.text: I recently got rear ended by an uninsured motorist not far from my home. I was able to speak with a specialist who gave me some common sense recommendations about getting evaluated instead of just "shaking it off" and hoping I was okay.  I was still dealing with the shock of it all and these folks reassured me that I would be just fine.  I'm grateful to have them in my corner.  So far, we've not needed any formal legal assistance; but, I sleep better knowing that I have a trusted attorney group in my corner just in case it comes to that.  So...big or small accident--Leave it to Schriever!

reviews.text: I’m extremely grateful for the work Preston did on my behalf. He fought to make sure I was able to maintain meaningful time with my kids, which was the most important thing to me. Throughout the entire process, he was professional, responsive, and truly had my best interests in mind. Thanks to his guidance and advocacy, I reached a very fair settlement. I highly recommend him to anyone going through a difficult family law situation.

Other reviews say they can’t recommend Preston enough, and I wish I could add something better — but they’re right. Getting a DUI was an awful experience, and I knew I needed a good attorney. What I got was so much more.

Preston felt like a friend throughout the entire process. He eased my embarrassment, supported and even cheered my recovery efforts, and genuinely cared. He took all the legal stress off my plate so I could focus on getting better — all while keeping things light with humor when I needed it most.

In the end, his personal touch was only exceeded by his professional ability. Preston knows exactly how to advocate for his clients. If you’re on the fence about hiring him, do yourself a favor and retain him. You won’t regret it.

Preston was able to help me get shared custody of my daughter.  He worked with me through some concerns I had and continues to provide support.  He was also very fair in his pricing.  Definitely recommended!

I recommend Preston to represent anyone with legal issues that fall into his area of legal expertise. With many clients, he always responds in a timely manner and answers questions or concerns you may have. He'll represent you to the fullest from beginning to end.

My daughter needed the advice, representation, and support of a lawyer in a family law case. Preston's Day delivered all of those things and more.

I'm so grateful for talking her case and handling a difficult situation in such a professional manner. I highly recommend him.
M. Owens

I cannot recommend Preston Day Law highly enough. My sister retained him to represent her in a child support/divorce decree modification case brought against her by her ex-husband. Her ex-husband had been dragging out this case in the legal system for over two years. During that time, we were unable to afford legal representation, and her ex-husband and his lawyer repeatedly used intimidation and scare tactics to try to get her to give in to all of his demands. She stood her ground and would not give in. Thank goodness.

With a trial date quickly approaching, Preston took the case on with very little time to prepare. He went above and beyond, doing all the necessary due diligence—and then some. We were able to engage his services with a very reasonable retainer, and he managed it carefully, ensuring each dollar was well-spent. In court, he was extremely prepared, professional, and calm, presenting compelling arguments as to why this case should never have been brought to court in the first place.

What sets Preston apart is his integrity, confidence without arrogance, and his ability to focus on the law rather than the heightened emotions that often accompany family law cases. Thanks to his expertise and dedication, my sister won her case.

If you are seeking a family law attorney who is knowledgeable, professional, and genuinely cares about his clients, Preston Day is the one to call.

I can’t say enough good things about Preston Day. I hired him pretty late in the process, and he immediately stepped in with confidence and responsiveness. He was honest and matter-of-fact, which really helped me manage my expectations and stay grounded during a stressful time.What stood out most to me was his presence in court—his trial experience and the way he presented himself made me feel like I was in very capable hands. He’s a straight shooter, intelligent, and respectful, not only with me but also with the other attorney involved. That professionalism went a long way.I truly appreciated how he balanced being supportive while keeping everything focused on the facts. Having him represent me gave me peace of mind, and I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him to anyone looking for a family law attorney who really knows what he’s doing.

I cannot recommend Preston Day enough! He handled my DUI case with the utmost professionalism, expertise, and dedication. From the very beginning, he provided clear guidance, kept me informed every step of the way, and fought tirelessly to achieve the best possible outcome. His attention to detail and deep understanding of the law truly set him apart. Thanks to his brilliant work, I was able to navigate this difficult situation with confidence. If you need a skilled and trustworthy attorney, Preston Day is the one to call!

This sleepy lawyer got me in deep troubles by failing to schedule hearing in Salt Lake City court. I paid the retention fees in full, $2500. After two months, I received an arrest warrant and suspension of driving privileges because of Mr. Day's goof.

The court clerk explained that there was no lawyer on my case or filing was made by Mr. Day. Then, Mr. Day explained that he never received a notification of hearing, when he never made one.

After I filed a credit dispute to get refund and submitted all documents to prove that Mr. Preston Day failed to honor the retention agreement, failed to return calls or file court papers, I started getting many phone calls from him and Nate, his secretary. I had already located a better qualified attorney in Jardine Law Office.
Preston is seriously so AMAZING. He helped me tremendously during my time of hardship and I can't thank him enough. If you need the best attorney out there, look no further. Thank you again, Preston!
Amazing customer service. The legal team was quick to answer the phone and was able to answer my questions and point me in the right direction to help my kids.
Preston is an excellent attorney who cares about his clients.

Preston was professional and extremely personable. I reached out to him after being arrested, and he was able to explain the court process and helped me secure a favorable verdict.  I am happy to recommend him to anyone I know who finds themselves in a similar situation!

He is a great lawyer and a very empathetic caring person.  I can't recommend him enough.  He helped me so much with both a divorce and a legal matter.  The only negative is that he can sometimes be a bit difficult to get a hold of.  He needs to hire a secretary. Lol  He does get back in a timely matter when it's urgent though.

Mr. Day was extremely easy to communicate with, answered all questions in a timely manner and effectively resolved my case without any problems. Even with short notice he was able to review all of the supporting documents, set a plan and represent me in court when needed.
Preston was very helpful during a difficult time. Great counselor!

Preston Day has been an absolute angel in my life. I called him three years ago to get some advice. After 25 years of marriage, I never ever thought I would be facing a divorce and I was scared to death. He gave some great advice and said, 'You don't need an attorney at this point." I called back a year later and he remembered me! Again, gave me sound advice and said I still didn't need an attorney. When the time came that I did need an attorney, I went with Preston and it was the best decision I could have made. Hiring him was worth every penny. I wish no one ever had to face the ugliness of divorce, but if you do, you want Preston in your corner. He helped me see that scary words mean nothing when you have the law on your side. He brought confidence and peace to my heart.

Let's be honest...attorneys are expensive. And many of them charge similar or the same rates. So who to go with? Let me tell you about my attorney.
Preston Day helped me navigate a difficult and complicated divorce. One that included negotiation, mediation, and multiple court sessions.
Preston provided guidance, assurance, and great care. The way he treated me and my family made me feel like we had someone in our corner at all times, who had our best interest and well-being as their priority.
What I also appreciated was that Preston did not hesitate to correct me if I misunderstood something, or needed to adjust during the course of the proceedings. I knew he did it to help me reach the best possible outcome, and trusted his guidance and correction where needed.
While of course I can't speak for other cases, I can speak for mine when I say that he helped me achieve a far better outcome than I could have ever hoped for, and helped me protect my children and myself.
I will be forever grateful for the role Preston played during this difficult time.
Super helpful! He will listen to your questions and give you detailed and thoughtful answers. I would highly recommend him for family law.

Preston was the best choice I could have made in finding an attorney to help me through a bad period in my life.
I am highly recommending him if you need the best possible representation.

He has a varied background in multiple areas of the law and clearly has a very broad knowledge base. I can't express how helpful he was to me during this one phone conversation. I was not nickel and dimed, but told exactly what the costs would be. I couldn't have had a more positive experience.

3 years ago, I was friends with a mentally disturbed woman. She abruptly ended the relationship and I had a panic attack that I didn’t handle well. It resulted in me being charged.

The case should have and could have easily been dismissed, but HD defended me very weakly. He will say that he did his best, however, a witness was in the room and witnessed HD fumble representation of me. The alleged victim gave a libelous testimony of events about me. I urged HD to rebut her or say that I disagree with her version of events. Instead, HD chose not to say anything.

I plead guilty because HD had manipulated me into thinking that I couldn’t win at all. However, studying the Utah law, I don’t see how my intent could have fallen into that law. I sincerely regret pleading guilty.

HD’s junior attorney ended up getting my misdemeanor reduced to the most simple misdemeanor. However, the damage is done.

I'm sad to change this review as I thought this was a great law firm at the time but in retrospect I really wish I'd gone with someone else. Looking back, there wasn't really an explanation on any discovery or why things went the way they did with the cases I had. He even told me he'd help me get an out of state criminal history report when it was time to submit it to the court a year later for a plea in abeyance. When I asked about it he just replied "I’m not sure. You’d have to check with Texas."
I was also curious on reopening cases to try and get them lowered/reevaluated since I got a 70% PTSD rating from the VA (for an event that happened 2 years before any cases and would be relevant to those cases) but apparently that's a $400 phone call for them.
These people know I am a veteran with PTSD struggling to find work and yet they only pretend to care when there's money involved. I guess the 15k I paid wasn't enough to warrant a call about those cases I already paid for. It's very insulting.

Edit after owners response:

I’m not sure why I have to go out of my way to call and try to make things right. You have my email.

reviews.text: Very professionally and carefully planned every aspect of our family estate planning. Every need for us was covered and brought to our attention for us to decide and be aware of. Can't say enough great things about RobRoy's knowledge and character.

Rob Roy consulted with us to carefully create an appropriate estate plan to precisely meet our needs and to formulate it in a manner that would provide for and protect everyone in the trust.  Rob Roy and Melissa were attentive to our needs.  They were very professional, yet empathetic in every aspect of trust development.  The ancillary staff was equally competent.  The process was smooth and effortless due to the entire Platt  Law team working to best serve the client.

We found that Platt Law is an excellent resourse and attorney for helping put together our Living Trust and Will plans.  We enjoyed our experience and their kindness in helping us through the process.  They were knowlegeable and able to help us determine the things we needed.  We would recommend them to anyone who is looking for Living Trust consultation.

Our experience with Platt Law was very pleasant. The firm is professional and pays close attention to detail.  Rob Roy was very caring and truly interested in us as individuals.  We are confident that our estate and legacy has been thoughtfully planned for.  Platt Law has our best interests at heart.

Effective estate planning requires the assistance of extremely bright legal counselors.  They must be versed in a broad range of the most complicated laws including the Internal Revenue Code, the Social Security Act, Medicare, Medicaid and the Employee Retirement Income Security Act.  As an attorney, I respect the complexity of these laws and refer my own clients to Platt Law whenever they have questions about estate planning, wealth preservation, veteran benefits, disability benefits and other elder law issues.  I have known the Platts for several years and know them to be extremely competent with and passionate about the services they provide.  I endorse them whole heartedly.

RobRoy and Platt Law, P.C. were great to work with. Rather than handing me a pile of boilerplate forms to fill out, RobRoy explained how estate plans, probate, wills, trusts worked and gave me options and recommendations for my circumstances. He fosters a long-term relationship that lends itself well to my changing needs rather than filling out a simple will that neither of us will look at again. His fees are reasonable and definitely worth the value of the service. RobRoy's staff is very professional and on top of my estate plan. I look forward to working with RobRoy for years to come.

I talked to RobRoy about some of my concerns with estate planning and setting up a will and trust that ensures that the government doesn't get all the stuff I want my kids to have.  RobRoy reminded me that Legal Zoom can only take you so far.

I was also pleased to see that their knowledge also spans through business formation and liability issues.

The people at Platt Law are knowledgeable about everything related to estate planning and asset protection.  I recommend them for your family and business needs.
Eric is awesome! He takes pride in personal and careful attention to detail, and it shows.

I can’t even put into words how thankful I am for Eric. My case has been one of the most complex and emotionally heavy situations you can imagine. It’s been full of difficult legal issues, high conflict, and constant challenges, and he has been absolutely exceptional every step of the way.

From the very beginning, he’s been incredibly responsive, I’m talking every single call, text, and email answered, often within minutes. He’s fair, transparent, and unbelievably thorough. He didn’t just learn my case, he knew it inside and out, to the point that during trial he was pulling facts, dates, and details with perfect precision.

In trial, he was everything you want an attorney to be. He was confident, prepared, ethical, and strategic. He knew when to push, when to hold back, and exactly how to present my case. I could tell he genuinely cared, not just about winning, but about doing the right thing for my child and protecting what truly matters.

He treats you like you’re his only case, invests himself fully, he advocates and fights for you. On top of all that, he’s reasonable and fair with his pricing, which is almost unheard of for an attorney of his caliber.

If you’re looking for someone who will go to bat for you no matter how complicated your case is, who will keep you informed, who will be in your corner 100% of the time, and who will fight for the truth while staying ethical and honest, Eric really is it. I am beyond grateful to have had him on my side.

Highly recommend for legal work involving trusts.

I reached out to this law firm hoping for structured legal guidance. Unfortunately, my experience was marked lack of attention to detail. I had to send the same request six times, before receiving an answer I was looking for.

While Kevin appeared professional in tone (that I appreciated), the handling of my inquiry did not meet basic expectations. In my professional opinion, this was a sloppy job that could and should have been resolved with a single email. I’m genuinely unsure how a legal team can advise business clients without first understanding the full scope of the request.

I wish you good luck if you choose to work with this firm—but I felt it necessary to share my experience.
The prosecutor incorrectly cited my traffic charge. Evidence insufficient to prove I was violating the traffic because it was a post-stop video. I do not believe these guys qualify for their job.

Private Prosecutors that are not public officials.  I asked for discovery for a year and was given it 30 minutes before trial. The discovery letter sent back simply said we don't have to give discovery.
Great lawfirm

We hired Melissa K. Mellor with Hansen Law out of Pleasant Grove specifically thinking she would be aggressive and take on our case with enthusiasm and effort. We are trying to recover 7 million dollars in a fraud case. We were disappointed in every way. After we fired Ms. Mellor and hired another lawyer, we constructed a time-line of our interaction with her. Our correspondence when she did respond was, “Sorry for the delay,” “I’m sorry for the lack of progress on your case,” “I also apologize because my workload has prevented me from devoting the time necessary to your case,” and placing blame for lack of response and progress on personal issues (family emergency, vertigo and oral surgery). After a few weeks, we sent another email stating we were frustrated with the lack of communication and asked if we should find another attorney if she didn’t have the time for our case. She responded again, apologizing for the delay and said she was “confused from a previous email.” We find out from our new attorney that work we thought was done was never done and we were shocked to find out that Ms. Mellor gave the defendant options that were not in our best interest and basically sent us backwards and cost us valuable time to pursue justice. Her neglect, negligence and nonperformance after approximately ten months makes me question her ability to actually be a lawyer.

Cherylyn Egner in family/custody law was the worst expirence of my life. I signed on in November of last year. Consultation was fine but then nothing happened for 9 months until I messaged to try to get things going again, then I was completely ignored I emailed and left messages, and called several times. I ended up canceling my legal shield subscription and asking for my retainer back, and that’s when I got a response. Cost me nearly 1300 and a year of time, for literally nothing. Very unprofessional. Considered suing for gross negligence. I can’t speak about the other lawyers here but don’t use Cherylyn.
We are working on a trust with Hansen Law. This was our first meeting with Tucker, who was personable and knowledgeable. So far so good.

Tucker Hansen is the Prosecuting Attorney for Cottonwood Heights.  He is a private prosecutor that has no connection to the city of Cottonwood Heights.  He does not read the case before court, he wastes taxpayer money and time.  He will not return calls or emails.  Tucker is all about money and not doing what is right in the court of law.

Still working through my case, but Cherylyn and Melissa have been fantastic to work with and have done a wonderful job helping me, especially given the difficult circumstances of my situation, as well as my severe anxiety through everything.
they out right lie to you about what they deliver

I worked with Hansen Law to update my will. It was honestly a simple update that I needed to make, but became an ordeal when I was essentially forgotten and had to follow up with the attorney several times to get anything to move. The attorney was apologetic, but I don't expect this in a professional setting. I will not be returning.

Hansen Law is in control of a garnishment for my ex wife and I. They applied the total amount to both of our companies. Now I have another garnishment I am paying on so their was on hold for my company but my ex was not. The garnishment was paid in full according to my ex's pay stubs in May yet Hansen Law has not sent a release to either of our companies. This means they were about to garnish my paychecks even thought the debt has been paid.

When I called today to get clarification and a release sent over I was answered with "I will email someone your request." The request was sent over to a Dorothy and no response was made. I called a second time to tell them of the urgency I had and was told the information would be sent on. Once I finally did receive a restricted call back from Dorothy I was instantly told that I was being belligerent to the secretary (don't feel that I was) and they wont stand for that. Because of the perceived belligerence the call with Dorothy was very hostile the whole time. I was told to the extent that they were not sending a release to my company and blamed my company for the lack of communication. So it was insinuated that they will still be garnishing my check next week and once they get payment if the garnishment was already satisfied they will refund my amount.

The whole experience with this law firm was hostile and unprofessional in my opinion. I do not disagree with the garnishment as I am responsible for the loan that defaulted. But that is no reason to be hostile and rude to a person who is only looking for details for their own financial situation.
Laramie was so amazing to work with. He communicated well and always had our best interest in mind. I will always recommend him!

reviews.text: Another law office referred me to Hansen Wright for their knowledge and expertise regarding trademarks.  I was in the beginning stages of establishing a new company and needed advice on trademark basics.  The reception was warm and conversation comfortable, as they guided me through “Trademark 101”.  Hansen Wright understood my financial limitations and helped me prioritize the various actions that were needed.    For a very reasonable fee, they prepared and submitted my trademark application.  Most impressive was their ability to predict where it may have trouble, so it was no surprise when opposition arose.  As a financially-limited underdog I was overwhelmed at my position against an opposing deep-pocketed institution.  But, as an undying champion of the underdog, Tim Merrill offered a thoughtfully clever direction of focus.  He earnestly presented possible outcomes, and although he was hopeful, he prudently offered a backup plan.  I was comforted knowing that he could envision the point where time and money spent would no longer be fruitful.  We agreed on a plan, and Tim put it into action.  His work was straightforward and respectful.  His clever ideas transformed into carefully crafted statements and responses to all parties involved.  His work was brilliantly executed.  Today, I am happy to report that Tim Merrill’s efforts have proven successful! reviews.text: Great company until you need to cancel or place account on hold. I have worked with Legacy Legal on and off for a few years. Even when their rates were $59 a month. So I join up again in January of 2018- wasn't thrilled about the rates going up, but signed up anyways. After not seeing results and having state license department question my credit disputes for my employer I decided to cancel my account. I was talked into placing my account on hold. Ok Cool! until you FORGET (because life happens) to call to place your account on hold for another 3 months (sales tactic) and then get charged. I called this morning 5-6 times at 9am on the dot until 9:15am and no one answered- operator stated no agents available and the call was immediately disconnected. So I emailed them, at 5pm I had still NOT heard a response. So I had to call in again. Finally spoke to someone, obviously I'm upset that I had a missed call to my work phone number from them, but I left my personal cell # on the voicemail as a call back number. Do you think I got a call to my cell or a response to my email. Absolutely not. I was offered to send dispute letters wen I actually got a hold of someone. At this point for what!? I had not seen any movement to my credit file, except a deletion I DID  ON MY OWN during the 3 months my account was on hold. I was told I couldn't get a refund. So you're keeping my $79 for what exactly? Come to find out, back in July I was charged a $79 fee, apparently for not calling in on time to place my account on hold for another 3 months. I read the email and it NEVER stated to call in or you would get charged, it said if you "DIDN'T call in your account may be cancelled" which I wanted to do actually. Total opposite of what they tell you on the phone. So here I am needing to print out a document to mail it in to their office to actually cancel and close my account. NEVER AGAIN with this company.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Christa, we have loved serving you and your family over the years helping you get your credit scores up to buy your home, etc. I'm sorry to hear of your negative experience with being on hold. We provide this to our clients in order to help keep your price down and get right back to work on your credit. Unfortunately if things change for you and you forget to talk to us this can happen. We are always interested in keeping our clients happy, I would be glad to work with you on this. Otherwise we are sad to see you go unhappy.

reviews.text: I don't recommend using Legacy, as they take their time, of course on purpose before they do anything to your account, since you are paying them monthly.  Do not expect any fast results if you get any at all.  Plus they will rip you off of an extra payment, after requesting to cancel, stating they want you to send a letter thru the mail, which is another stall tactic to get more money.  Who sends in written requests in this day and time, their a joke!!!

Our Customer Service team, though they are not perfect do a great job in taking care of our clients and getting the results they are looking for. reviews.text: I've scheduled an appointment for each day, the last two days in a row to discuss a pressing matter where time is sensitive. I still haven't heard from anyone. Kind of an indicator of what service would be like after retention. I'll continue my search.
reviews.ownerResponse: After reviewing the matter, and speaking with all the relevant members of our office, it is clear that you did not schedule any appointments with anyone in our office, but simply requested a return call from our family law attorney. You were informed that he was out of the office at a mediation on the day you called, and would call you when he returned to the office later the next day. You indicated that you were fine with this, and would await his call. You then called again early the next morning when the attorney was still out on the matter from the prior day. You were again informed that he would contact you later that day when he finished the matter he was working on outside of the office, and that he would give your matter as much attention as he does his other matters. You were informed that if you felt that time sensitivity made it so you couldn't wait for his return call later that day, that you should contact another attorney. You indicated that you felt you needed to do that, and therefore, would not need our attorney's return call. Sometimes cases that we are working on, in court, or in mediations, don't allow us the opportunity to immediately call people back while we are engaged in those other matters. As with you, our office staff informs the caller of this immediately, so that they know what to expect, and can decide for themselves how they would like to proceed. I sincerely hope that you were able to find someone to help you with your legal matter. It is rare that it takes 24 hours to return a person's call, but in those situations, we make sure you understand the circumstances so that you can proceed as you need.

Brady Brammer is a lying POS that has no problem resorting to illegal means (Having private hearings without making an effort to notify the opposition) to win cases. I will be making a bar complaint as well!
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Stidham is a disgruntled opposing party, not a client. He bitterly lost a suit to our client and he paid for it. In an attempt to avoid a business debt, he removed his name from his business. Our firm noticed all hearings properly, but because Mr. Stidham was no longer an owner, he was not entitled to receive notice. In the end, Mr. Stidham’s deception failed and he paid the debt. If you are in a situation where an opposing party isn’t playing fair, contact us.

reviews.text: We were at a loss as to what to do to recover our loan investment.  We. at the last minute called and talked to Brady Brammer.  He indicated that we did have some options and assisted us in recovering almost all of our funds that we had thought was a total loss.  Brady Brammer is a fantastic lawyer, professional, knowledgeable, dependable and fought for us.  What a great find!  We recommend Brady and Spaulding Law 110%.

Our company was privileged to work with both Seth Gomm and Brady Brammer, and I would recommend both of them without any reservation.  Their knowledge of the law, as well as the process of dealing with difficult circumstances, combined with their knack for explaining things in a clear and concise way made all the difference and was invaluable to our team.  They were patient, thorough, understanding, and did not apply any pressure to decisions that our team was to consider.  Spaulding Law is an excellent firm and we will definitely be utilizing their services for all of our future legal needs.

I honestly could not recommend a better guy than Brady Brammer. He was nothing short of professional and amazing in helping me with my circumstance. If you are in the position to need an attorney then Brady Brammer is the one you need to go to. I can't say enough good things about him. His counsel is clear and his depth of understanding is excellent. I am very impressed with his quality of work. You will not regret working with Seth and his firm.
Spaulding Gomm does great work! They are very responsive and have a solid team.

For two years Spaulding Gomm has provided dedicated service to our rapidly-growing, family-owned business in Denver, CO. Supporting our corporate vision by guiding us from a small business to a successful LLC, Mr. Gomm has assisted us with our entire corporate restructuring. His offices has provided outstanding service, as he advised us on the creation of parent companies, completing trademark applications and protecting our intellectual property, as well as overseeing the expansion of our commercial real estate assets. We are so grateful to have a trusted advisor who is responsive, accessible and willing to travel to meet our legal needs.

With his vast understanding of the complex regulatory nature of our specific industry, Mr. Gomm has created a long-lasting relationship with us by continuing to understand the scope of our legal needs and referring us to other reputable legal resources, when helpful. By focusing on the areas of expertise that Spaulding Gomm is known for they have catapulted our business forward in our growth and expansion through drafting non-compete, confidentiality, and employment agreements that protect us and our valuable knowledgebase. Through providing estate planning services, developing trusts and exploring succession planning, as our business continues to grow, we can ensure that our hard work provides a legacy for our loved ones for generations to come.

We would like to thank Spaulding Gomm for their ongoing partnership with our company and supporting our ability to serve others and live our mission each day. Having trusted, reliable, responsive legal counsel has enabled us to focus on growth, success, and providing quality service. We look forward to many years of partnership and shared prosperity.

FRIENDLY; EFFICIENT; COURTEOUS; CUSTOMER ORIENTED.

I hired Peter Kern six months ago to help me with some custody matters. He has excellent online reviews, but my experience with Peter has been disappointing, to say the least. He helped me with three hearings and a mediation and only one of those had somewhat of a favorable outcome for me. He was extremely underprepared on my last hearing and it resulted in me losing the case and having to pay the other party’s attorney fees. Upon receiving Peter’s invoices, it seemed that his approach was to nickel and dime me, including charging me for brief text messages with his assistant. His response time on emails was so delayed that the response was often no longer helpful. He also had many phone issues that frequently disrupted our phone calls, resulting in more time and expense. I’m not sure if Peter has too many clients and this was the reason for his last-minute work and lack of preparation. It astonishes me to have paid so much money to Peter Kern over the last six months and to have received so little value from his legal services. I cannot recommend him.
